title: Huazhong University of Science and Technology
subtitle: Bachelor's degree in Electronic and Information Engineering, 2016-2020
summary: I spent four years for my undergraduate study in Wuhan, China. Originally I was in the School of Environment but later transferred to the School of Electronic Information and Communications. We have a huge campus with lush trees, making the summer feels pleasant rather than tortuous like other places in Wuhan.

blocks.text: I graduated in the top 10 with a 3.91/4.0 GPA, toped the class in the end but never got to top any single semester, so it was funny.

The first two years were occupied by trying to have fun while keeping up with the courses. I did tour most places in Wuhan that I found interesting and actively participated in Poestry Association and SICA (Student International Communication Association), and tried to found a Spanish club but failed.

The thrid year came with the burden to lay bedrock for the future, as I tried to get admitted to a pre-graduate program summer school, I did not find myself lucky when other students were talking about research and projects, yet the only thing I could talk was reading.

So I came back to the school in the summer of 2019 to do some research, when I picked the adversarial machine learning as the domain by accident and nurtured a published paper eventually (but it won't be officially published until 2021).
In the first month of 2020, the world gets paused and I had to postpone my plan to study abroad. We did not even get a graduation ceremony because of it.
